metadata.title: A2 Key Gold Mock 3
metadata.description: M4.1 academic mock for A2 Key — education, entertainment, and social themes across reading, writing, listening, and speaking.
metadata.authoringNotes: M4.1 Gold Mock — manually authored and academically reviewed.

parts.title: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct picture
parts.instructions: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct picture

audio.transcript: Teacher: Please write the answer on the board so everyone can see it. Student: OK, I'll write the example now.
audio.caption: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct picture

parts.title: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer
parts.instructions: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer

audio.transcript: Boy: I couldn't do the maths test because I was ill. Teacher: Don't worry — you can take it next week.
audio.caption: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer

parts.title: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap
parts.instructions: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap

audio.transcript: Organiser: The lecture begins at eleven o'clock. Let's meet at the school gate. The exhibition closes at half past five.
audio.caption: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap

audio.transcript: Speaker: My favourite subject is biology, and I joined the film club at the start of this term. I enjoy group projects because I like sharing ideas.
audio.caption: Part 4 — Listen to the monologue

parts.title: Part 5 — Listen and match each day to an activity
parts.instructions: Part 5 — Listen and match each day to an activity

audio.transcript: Coordinator: Debate club is on Monday, film screening Tuesday, poetry reading Wednesday, games night Thursday, and the talent show on Friday.
audio.caption: Part 5 — Listen and match each day to an activity

questions.questionText: Exam results available online from 9 a.m. Friday.

choices.text: Students can check results on the website on Friday morning.
Results are only given in person.

questions.questionText: Cinema — Student tickets half price on weekdays.

choices.text: Students pay less from Monday to Friday.
All tickets are free for students.
Prices are higher at the weekend.

questions.questionText: Youth club — New members welcome. Join us on Thursdays.

choices.text: Anyone can become a member and meet on Thursdays.
The club is only for existing members.

questions.questionText: Science fair — Projects must be submitted by 3 p.m. on 12 May.

choices.text: Students need to hand in projects before the deadline.
Projects can be submitted any time.

questions.questionText: Concert tonight — doors open 7 p.m., show starts 8 p.m.

choices.text: The performance begins at eight o'clock.

questions.questionText: Charity fun run — Registration closes 48 hours before the event.

choices.text: You cannot register in the last two days before the run.
Registration is open until the race starts.
The event is only for charity workers.

Who plays the violin in the school orchestra?

pairs.rightText: practises with the orchestra every Friday

pairs.rightText: is planning a birthday celebration for her cousin

pairs.rightText: travelled to the capital for a live music festival

questions.questionText: Who helps younger students with maths homework?

pairs.rightText: volunteers in the after-school tutoring club

questions.questionText: Who is going to perform in a street theatre festival?

pairs.rightText: is rehearsing a comedy sketch with his classmates

questions.questionText: Who has created a social media group for new students?

pairs.rightText: has invited classmates to share advice online

questions.questionText: When does the drama club meet?
questions.passage: Our school drama club is preparing a comedy for the end-of-term show. Twenty students from Years 9 and 10 meet every Wednesday after lessons in the hall. The drama teacher, Ms Ortiz, has written a script about a group of friends who enter a talent competition. Rehearsals started six weeks ago, and the students have already learned most of their lines. They are now practising dances and simple lighting changes. Tickets will go on sale next Monday for parents and local residents. The club hopes to raise money for new costumes and sound equipment. Everyone is excited because this is the first live performance since the school reopened the theatre.

questions.questionText: Gap (1): patience and regular ___

content.passage: Learning a musical instrument takes patience and regular (1) ___. Beginners should practise for short periods every day rather than one long session at the (2) ___. It helps to play pieces you enjoy, but your teacher may also give you exercises to improve your (3) ___. When you perform in front of others, try to stay calm and listen carefully to the (4) ___. Joining a school band or orchestra is a good way to meet people who share your (5) ___. Remember that progress may be slow at first, but consistent effort usually leads to better (6) ___ over time.

questions.questionText: Write an email to your English teacher about school.
questions.prompt: Your class is organising a charity concert at school. Write an email to your English teacher.
questions.taskDescription: Write 25–35 words. Include all three points.
requiredPoints: Say whether you can help before the concert
Explain one job you could do
Ask what time you should arrive on the day

content.prompt: Your class is organising a charity concert at school. Write an email to your English teacher.
content.taskDescription: Write 25–35 words. Include all three points.
requiredPoints: Say whether you can help before the concert
Explain one job you could do
Ask what time you should arrive on the day

questions.questionText: Write a story about meeting a new friend.
questions.prompt: Your English teacher has asked you to write a story. Your story must begin with this sentence: 'I was nervous before the school party.'

questions.prompt: The examiner will ask you about your school, your favourite subjects, and how you spend time with friends.

How do you usually relax after a busy week?
Tell me about a club or activity you belong to.
